conbody.p: This section describes issues and limitations present in Juniper Cloud-Native Router (JCNR) release 24.3.

section.title: Known Issues and Limitations in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 24.3

li.p: JCNR-10074: The maximum number of labels in a label-stack for an interface is limited to three—By default, the maximum number of labels in a label-stack for an interface is three. When the label-stack depth exceeds the maximum number of labels, the backup path for fast re-route (FRR) is unavailable. You cannot increase the number of labels on an interface currently.

JCNR-9989: Traffic drop at ingress L3 sub-interface with multifield (MF) classifier applied—Traffic drop may be observed when L3 CoS filter with MF classifier is applied on an ingress sub-interface. Restarting the vRouter pod fixes the issue.

JCNR-8498: JCNR Forwarding plane not fragmentating VLAN-tagged frames with size greater than 8968 bytes— The JCNR forwarding plane does not fragment VLAN-tagged frames with size greater than 8968 bytes. The frames are currently dropped.
JCNR-6549: SSH login authentication failure to JCNR controller (cRPD) results in zero bytes core files—When accessing cRPD via SSH, login authentication failures result in zero byte core files.
